spec: Holo Files (ADR-0058) — the native, substrate-native file explorer: one window onto the OS's content-addressed object universe + a writable OPFS Home. It auto-detects the host OS and wears its native chrome (Finder/Explorer/Files/Nautilus); every object carries its did:holo κ and re-derives on demand (Law L5 — a tampered byte is refused); Home is read/WRITE while the substrate is read-only; a core part of the World shell. SEAMLESSLY integrates Holo Search (a unified omnibox: recursive local search + paste-a-κ/identifier resolve + open-web answer) and Holo Cloud (it mounts the SAME content-addressed OPFS κ-store the Cloud app uses, so a file sent from Files appears in Holo Cloud — one substrate, not a bridge).
authority: W3C File System Access / OPFS (navigator.storage.getDirectory) · W3C Web Cryptography (SHA-256 re-derivation) · WHATWG Fetch + DOM · W3C UA Client Hints (host detection, holo-platform) · Nextcloud WebDAV (holo-webdav, Holo Cloud) · the unified-window resolve/federate/answer pipeline (Holo Search, no AI) · UOR-ADDR (κ = H(canonical_form)) · holospaces Laws L1/L3/L4/L5 · files-community/Files UX (reproduced) · real Chromium via Playwright

results.name: the explorer boots and paints its Home landing page
auto-detects the host OS and wears its native chrome (implicit, not stated in the window)
clicking Holospaces lists every app from the catalog
a holospace opens to its sealed closure (the _shared runtime folder is present)
selecting a file shows its content address (did:holo κ) in the details pane
Verify re-derives the file and its content address matches (Law L5)
dragging a file out carries its holo://κ content address
a tampered content address is REFUSED — the bytes do not re-derive to it
New folder writes into the OPFS Home and the listing reflects it
a file written to Home reads back byte-identical (read/WRITE substrate)
a new tab opens (multi-tab browsing)
switching tabs activates the clicked tab
closing a tab returns to a single tab
Ctrl-click extends a multi-selection
Home entries are draggable (drag-and-drop enabled)
a file moves into a folder (the drag-to-move primitive)
a colored tag is assigned to a file and persists
the tag surfaces in the sidebar Tags section (filterable)
dual-pane opens a second, independent pane
the second pane navigates independently of the first
the sort/group menu groups the listing (group headers render)
a Status column distinguishes local (working copy) from sealed (κ-pinned)
a .zip compresses and extracts byte-identically (real DEFLATE via Compression Streams)
the unified search box finds objects across the substrate (Holo Search · local plane)
a pasted content address (κ) resolves to its object locally — the omnibox is a window onto every object
dropping a κ link in materializes its object into Home (drop-to-materialize)
Send to Holo Cloud chunks + content-addresses the file into the shared κ-store
the file appears in the Holo Cloud location — seamless, one substrate (not a bridge)
the cloud copy re-derives to its content address (Law L5 over the cloud κ-store)
no fatal console errors across the whole flow
